preset_description: Dark = identity signal. Visual language expresses who the user is. Validator gets authority and weight (heavy dark). Mover gets minimal light with one dark CTA punch. Explorer gets all-neutral — open, spacious, no urgency.

brief.audience: Startups seeking an ai-first, intent-driven design system

accent_tokens._note: Brand primary CTA color. Global — not per-preset. Two variants: button on a light/neutral section vs. on a dark section.

beats.name: Orientation
beats.rationale: Establishes immediate context: MODE is an AI-first, intent-driven design system built for startups. Frames the page for the Validator archetype by clearly stating what this is and who it's for, anchoring the decision-stage visitor who already has awareness.

beats.name: Credibility
beats.rationale: Validators need trust signals before they'll engage with claims. Placing social proof, customer logos, and authority markers early satisfies their instinct to verify legitimacy and reduces skepticism before deeper content. Archetype guidance requires this beat.

beats.name: Evidence
beats.rationale: Decision-stage visitors and Validators both demand concrete proof. Presenting metrics, case study data, and measurable outcomes here gives the hard evidence this archetype needs to justify their choice rationally before seeing pricing.

beats.name: Decision
beats.rationale: Required at decision funnel stage. Now that credibility and evidence have built a proof foundation, the pricing and plan comparison beat lets the Validator compare options and frame their final choice with confidence.

beats.name: Conversion
beats.rationale: Immediately follows Decision as required. The Validator has been given proof, trust signals, and clear plan options — this beat captures the commitment with a decisive CTA to convert to paid.

beats.name: Recovery
beats.rationale: Always last. Provides secondary paths for Validators who need more time — links to documentation, case studies, or a demo request — ensuring no visitor exits without an alternative route back into the funnel.

sections.name: Navigation
sections.rationale: Global navigation — wayfinding and persistent CTA for the session.

sections.name: Hero
sections.rationale: Establishes immediate context: MODE is an AI-first, intent-driven design system built for startups. Frames the page for the Validator archetype by clearly stating what this is and who it's for, anchoring the decision-stage visitor who already has awareness.

sections.name: Social proof
sections.rationale: Validators need trust signals before they'll engage with claims. Placing social proof, customer logos, and authority markers early satisfies their instinct to verify legitimacy and reduces skepticism before deeper content. Archetype guidance requires this beat.

sections.name: Proof points
sections.rationale: Decision-stage visitors and Validators both demand concrete proof. Presenting metrics, case study data, and measurable outcomes here gives the hard evidence this archetype needs to justify their choice rationally before seeing pricing.

sections.name: Pricing
sections.rationale: Required at decision funnel stage. Now that credibility and evidence have built a proof foundation, the pricing and plan comparison beat lets the Validator compare options and frame their final choice with confidence.

sections.name: CTA
sections.rationale: Immediately follows Decision as required. The Validator has been given proof, trust signals, and clear plan options — this beat captures the commitment with a decisive CTA to convert to paid.

sections.name: Footer
sections.rationale: Page close — brand mark, minimal navigation, and legal.

page.reasoning: Decision stage requires conversion focus. Notes specify: 'minimal variant for conversion-focused pages — strip nav_links, keep logo and single CTA only.'

Decision stage conversion requires minimal distraction. Notes state: 'For conversion stage use text-only: slim title and subhead only, no media, no secondary CTA — orients the visitor without competing with the decision moment below.'

slots.headline: Your design system knows how to render. MODE teaches it why.
slots.subhead: MODE is the semantic intelligence layer that resolves component behavior, visual expression, and copy register to the right experience — by archetype, funnel stage, and context. No manual variants. No QA overhead.
slots.trust_signal: One-time purchase. Lifetime access. No subscription.

page.reasoning: Validator archetype requires trust signals with attribution. Notes specify: 'Always include role and company for Validator credibility' and 'with-photo increases engagement and perceived authenticity.'

slots.quote: We were spending two to three days per launch producing variant pages for different audience segments. MODE collapsed that to a single brief. The resolution engine just makes the right call — we stopped second-guessing it after the first week.

page.reasoning: Validator archetype demands credibility reinforcement. Notes state: 'Always pair stats with source attribution in Validator contexts — unsourced stats reduce trust.'

slots.headline: What teams measure after deploying MODE.

stats.label: Median time from install to first working output
Page variants produced per brief vs. manual workflow
Reduction in design-to-dev handoff cycles reported by early adopters

page.reasoning: Decision stage conversion requires clear recommendation path. Notes indicate: 'highlighted variant for recommended plan' and 'Validator variant includes description and longer feature list.'

slots.description: The full intelligence layer. Everything you need to deploy semantic, intent-aware design system behavior in your own codebase — no subscriptions, no vendor dependency.
features: Semantic resolution engine — 4-dimension model (archetype, funnel, intent, context)
IA planner + multi-variant generation (Mover, Validator, Explorer in parallel)
Three palette presets — funnel-driven, archetype-driven, feature-emphasis
Token dashboard with editable palette map
Lifetime access — no runtime dependency, fork and extend freely

page.reasoning: Validator archetype at decision stage requires trust signal. Notes specify: 'Validator variant: include subhead and trust_signal (e.g., 'No credit card required').'

slots.headline: The decision is low-risk. The cost of building without it isn't.
slots.subhead: One purchase. Lives in your codebase. Running in under an hour. No lock-in, no subscription, no vendor to depend on.
slots.trust_signal: One-time payment · Lifetime access · No credit card traps

page.reasoning: Decision stage conversion requires recovery mechanism. Notes state: 'with-cta variant recaptures departing users on key conversion pages — use on pricing and signup flows.'
